scope.target: Retire legacy compatibility wrappers after reference migration is complete
scope.dependency_radius: Legacy script entry points plus script catalog documentation
constraints: Remove only wrappers that have no remaining repo references
Keep .NET-first names as the only documented entry points
non_goals: No changes to the canonical .NET script implementations

inputs.architecture_target: Finish the naming convergence by removing wrapper-only legacy script names once migration is complete
review_findings: Legacy wrapper names no longer have repo-internal references
Canonical .NET names are already the real implementation owners
user_constraints: Continue directly into the next stage
Remove wrappers that are no longer needed

steps: Verified that the three legacy script names have no remaining repo references
Deleted the wrapper-only legacy script files
Updated script README to remove compatibility-wrapper messaging
validation: No repo references remain to the retired legacy script names
Canonical .NET script names remain present and documented
Script inventory no longer lists the retired wrappers
deferred_items: Retire any other historical script names only after the same reference audit is completed

results.summary: Retired the three legacy wrapper-only script names after confirming the repo no longer references them, and updated the script catalog to document only the canonical .NET entry points.
